text: Bicycloalkanes, particularly cubanes and their structural analogues, have gained significant traction as non-classical bioisosteres for (hetero)arenes in medicinal chemistry. In response to the growing demand for these scaffolds, the synthetic community has pioneered a wide array of innovative methodologies. This review evaluates recent advancements in the light-driven construction and functionalization of these high-value molecules. We categorize these developments based on their structural targets, highlighting photochemical processes that provide access to [1.1.1]-bicyclopentanes (BCPs), [2.1.1]-bicyclohexanes (BCHs), [3.1.1]-bicycloheptanes (BCHeps), and cubanes, alongside other related bridged systems. Finally, we offer a perspective on the future of the field, focusing on the identification of novel reaction manifolds required to unlock next-generation functionalized bioisosteric units.
